meaning: adj.污秽的,邪恶的,肮脏的,淤塞的,恶劣的vt.弄脏,妨害,污蔑,犯规,淤塞vi.腐烂,犯规,缠结n.犯规,缠绕adv.违反规则地,不正当地
examples: Foul play will be severely penalized.
He had fallen foul of the FBI.
Six weeks of training wear away the foul speech, double negatives and incorrect tenses.
But owning a home, being married or having children complicates estate planning and increases the risk of foul-ups.
